skill.name: lark-workflow-standup-report
skill.description: Generates daily standup reports by combining calendar events and pending tasks from Lark. Automatically aggregates schedule and todo items for specified dates.
skill.summary: Automated standup report generator that combines Lark calendar events with pending tasks to create concise daily briefings.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 37 patterns but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only showing CLI usage examples. External command risk exists only because the skill uses lark-cli tool which requires authentication. No executable code or injection vectors present.

low_findings.title: External CLI Tool Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires lark-cli binary to be installed and authenticated. External commands execute with user privileges.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Requires bins: lark-cli in metadata - external dependency that must be trusted

content.user_title: Generate daily standup reports from Lark calendar and tasks
content.value_statement: Team members need quick visibility into their daily schedule and pending work. This skill combines calendar events with task lists to produce structured standup summaries automatically.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve calendar events for specified date ranges using lark-cli calendar +agenda
Fetch pending tasks assigned to current user via lark-cli task +get-my-tasks
Convert Unix timestamps to local time format with timezone awareness
Detect scheduling conflicts by analyzing overlapping event times
Generate formatted summary tables with RSVP status and task priorities
limitations: Requires lark-cli binary installation and prior authentication with calendar and task scopes
Date parameters must use ISO 8601 format - natural language dates like tomorrow are not supported
Task queries default to 20 items maximum unless page-all flag is used
Only supports user identity authentication - bot accounts not supported

use_cases.title: Daily standup preparation
use_cases.description: Generate a morning briefing showing today's meetings and pending tasks before team standup
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers preparing for daily scrum meetings

use_cases.title: Weekly planning review
use_cases.description: Review the week's calendar commitments and outstanding tasks to prioritize work
use_cases.target_user: Project managers coordinating team schedules and deadlines

use_cases.title: New day orientation
use_cases.description: Quick overview of today's schedule when starting work or returning from time off
use_cases.target_user: Remote workers managing async schedules across time zones

prompt_templates.title: Basic today summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my schedule and tasks for today
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick morning check of today's commitments

prompt_templates.title: Tomorrow preview
prompt_templates.prompt: What meetings and deadlines do I have tomorrow
prompt_templates.scenario: Evening planning to prepare for next day

prompt_templates.title: Weekly overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a standup report for this week from Monday to Friday
prompt_templates.scenario: Weekly planning session requiring full week visibility

prompt_templates.title: Custom date range
prompt_templates.prompt: Show calendar events and pending tasks from 2026-04-01 to 2026-04-07
prompt_templates.scenario: Specific date range analysis for sprint planning

output_examples.input: Show me my schedule and tasks for today
output_examples.output:
## Today's Summary (2026-04-01 Wednesday)

### Schedule
| Time | Event | Organizer | Status |
|------|-------|-----------|--------|
| 09:00-10:00 | Product Requirements Review | Zhang San | Accepted |
| 14:00-15:00 | Technical Design Discussion | Li Si | Pending |

### Tasks
- [ ] Complete API documentation (Due: 2026-04-02)
- [ ] Review pull request #142 (Due: 2026-04-01)

### Summary
- 2 meetings, 2 pending tasks
- No scheduling conflicts detected
- Free time slots: 10:00-14:00, 15:00-18:00

## Tomorrow's Summary (2026-04-02 Thursday)

### Schedule
| Time | Event | Organizer | Status |
|------|-------|-----------|--------|
| 10:00-11:00 | Sprint Planning | Team Lead | Accepted |

### Tasks
- [ ] Deploy staging environment (Due: 2026-04-02) - Overdue
- [ ] Update test coverage (No deadline)

### Summary
- 1 meeting, 2 pending tasks
- 1 task is overdue and needs attention

best_practices: Always use --due-end flag when fetching tasks to limit results to relevant deadlines
Verify calendar event RSVP status before reporting - declined events should not count as busy time
Check for timezone conversions when displaying times for users in different regions
anti_patterns: Do not fetch all historical tasks without filtering - can exceed context limits with 100KB+ responses
Avoid natural language date parsing - the CLI only accepts ISO 8601 format timestamps
Do not include declined meetings in conflict detection calculations

faq.question: What authentication is required before using this skill?
faq.answer: Run lark-cli auth login --domain calendar,task to authenticate with the required scopes before generating reports.

faq.question: Can I use natural language dates like tomorrow or next Monday?
faq.answer: No, the CLI only accepts ISO 8601 format dates. The AI will calculate the appropriate date range from natural language requests.

faq.question: How many tasks are returned by default?
faq.answer: The default query returns up to 20 tasks assigned to you. Use --page-all to retrieve all tasks or --due-end to filter by deadline.

faq.question: What happens if I decline a meeting?
faq.answer: Declined meetings are still displayed but marked as declined and excluded from conflict detection and busy time calculations.

faq.question: Can this skill work with bot accounts?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports user identity authentication. Bot accounts are not supported for calendar and task operations.

faq.question: How are tasks without deadlines handled?
faq.answer: Tasks without deadlines appear at the end of the sorted list. For summary reports, only tasks created within the last 30 days are shown.
